<!-- Financial-style table whose tail is a run of annotation rows: consecutive
     single-cell footnote lines in the stub column, then a full-width colspan
     note. None of them groups a data row, so none may be promoted to a
     rowgroup divider (an empty-extent rowgroup drops its label silently — the
     0.6.1 regression class); each must survive as a label-preservation rule,
     including the full-width colspan row that has no <td> to anchor on. -->
<table>
<tr><td></td><td>2017</td><td>2016</td></tr>
<tr><td>Profit per share 1</td><td>$1.26</td><td>$(0.11)</td></tr>
<tr><td>Adjusted profit per share 2</td><td>$6.88</td><td>$3.42</td></tr>
<tr><td>1 At statutory tax rates prior to reform.</td><td></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>2 Net of federal tax at 35 percent.</td><td></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td colspan="3">Per share amounts computed using fully diluted shares outstanding.</td></tr>
</table>
